source_quote: At 6 months the intervention group had a greater reduction of 0.24 units in BMI SDS than standard care (−0.23 vs. 0.01, p = 0.002).
reasoning: Mean difference -0.24 in BMI SDS change. Intervention=-0.23, control=0.01. No SDs provided.

source_quote: Results indicated a clinically significant treatment effect for boys on BMI (p < 0.001) but not girls.
reasoning: Only p-value provided, no numerical BMI values.

source_quote: No significant differences in weight were found between IT, sLMP and control groups.
reasoning: No numerical BMI values provided, only qualitative statement.

source_quote: The results showed that the intervention group reduced more body mass index (BMI)... but did not reach a statistically significant level
reasoning: No numerical BMI z-score values provided, only qualitative comparison.

source_quote: UE function (-9.8 ± 15.2, p = 0.04) improved in the IG compared to no change in the CG.
reasoning: Mean difference in DASH (disability) score = -9.8. The ±15.2 appears to be SE or pooled SD. Raw group means not provided.

source_quote: We were able to calculate sample estimates for candidate primary outcomes and test procedures...
reasoning: No numerical disability values provided in results_text excerpt.

source_quote: All adolescents (n = 161; BMI ≥95th%, age 16 ± 2.5 year) lost weight over 24-weeks (−1.29%, [−1.82, −0.76], p < 0.0001), with no significant weight loss difference between groups (p = 0.3).
reasoning: Overall %BMIp95 change = -1.29% [95% CI -1.82, -0.76], but no between-group difference. This is overall effect, not intervention vs control.

source_quote: Group B receiving individualized cardio rehab showed significant improvements... Moreover, Group B exhibited enhanced cardiovascular fitness...
reasoning: No numerical systolic BP values provided in results_text excerpt, only qualitative comparison.

source_quote: The estimated median difference in the six-minute walking test was in favour of the intervention group by 30 metres (95% CI, 55 to 1; effect size 0.64; P = 0.037)... There were no differences between groups... in 10-metres walking time.
reasoning: Study reports 6-minute walk test (30m difference) and mentions "no difference" in 10-meter walk time, but does not provide numerical walking speed (m/s) values.

source_quote: Combined exercise group: SBP (mmHg) Pre 139.80±13.90, Post 126.20±7.82, p=0.022. Conventional group: SBP Pre 136.50±8.00, Post 142.13±8.00.
reasoning: Extracted POST-treatment SBP values. Combined exercise (intervention) = 126.20±7.82 mmHg, Conventional (control) = 142.13±8.00 mmHg.

source_quote: functional outcomes (HHS: DLA 83 points vs DAA 87 points; p = 0,71).
reasoning: Harris Hip Score (functional status): DAA (intervention) = 87 points, DLA (control) = 83 points. No SDs provided. p=0.71 (not significant).

source_quote: At 6 months, 3 of 7 (42.9%) participants in the NRT + behavioral support arm, 6 of 8 (75%) vape-taper + behavioral support arm, and 4 of 9 (44.4%) self-guided arm self-reported being vape-free and nicotine-free.
reasoning: Vaping cessation at 6 months: vape-taper+behavioral (intervention) 6/8 (75%), self-guided (control) 4/9 (44.4%).

source_quote: The mortality was higher in the AMIS-group.
reasoning: States mortality was higher in AMIS group, but no numerical counts (events/n) provided in results_text excerpt.

source_quote: Results section describes baseline demographics and EC use behaviors but does not report cessation outcomes at 3-6 months.
reasoning: No cessation outcome data provided in the results_text excerpt. Only baseline and recruitment data shown.

source_quote: Primary outcome: little evidence of a difference in OHS at 120 days; adjusted mean difference (SPAIRE—lateral) −1.23 (95% CI −3.96 to 1.49, p=0.37).
reasoning: Oxford Hip Score (HRQoL) at 120 days: adjusted MD = -1.23 [95% CI -3.96, 1.49], p=0.37. Not significant.
